title: Cerasus × subhirtella
intro: Cerasus × subhirtella, commonly known as the Japanese flowering cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The health benefits of Cerasus × subhirtella include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential therapeutic applications in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cerasus × subhirtella include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cerasus × subhirtella is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a cross between Cerasus jamasakura and Cerasus serrulata. The classification of Cerasus × subhirtella is as a nothospecies, a type of hybrid species that is intermediate in characteristics between its parent species. The nothospecies classification acknowledges the plant's mixed ancestry and hybrid nature. This classification system helps to distinguish Cerasus × subhirtella from its parent species, which are native to Japan.
uses: Cerasus × subhirtella, also known as Japanese flowering cherry, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its bark and leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In addition, it is also used to treat gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions.
